| `revision` | [`semantic_version`](#semantic-version) | ✓ | Revision number |

<details>
<summary><a id="spec-state"></a><strong><code>spec_state</code></strong> — Ratification state of a specification or extension version: `development` (actively being worked on), `frozen` (feature-complete, under review), `public-review` (open for public comment), `ratification-ready` (approved by the task group, awaiting board vote), `ratified` (officially approved by RISC-V International), or `nonstandard-released` (released but not part of the RISC-V standard).</summary>

Ratification state of a specification or extension version: `development` (actively being worked on), `frozen` (feature-complete, under review), `public-review` (open for public comment), `ratification-ready` (approved by the task group, awaiting board vote), `ratified` (officially approved by RISC-V International), or `nonstandard-released` (released but not part of the RISC-V standard).

**Allowed values:**

- `development`
- `frozen`
- `public-review`
- `ratification-ready`
- `ratified`
- `nonstandard-released`

</details>

<details>
<summary><a id="extension-version"></a><strong><code>extension_version</code></strong> — Exact version in `MAJOR[.MINOR[.PATCH]]` format (e.g., `2.1.0`, `2.1`, `2`)</summary>

Exact version in `MAJOR[.MINOR[.PATCH]]` format (e.g., `2.1.0`, `2.1`, `2`)

</details>

<details>
<summary><a id="requirement-string"></a><strong><code>requirement_string</code></strong> — Version requirement string (e.g., `&gt;= 2.0`, `~&gt; 1.5`, `= 2.1`). The `~&gt;` operator is a pessimistic version constraint: `~&gt; X.Y` means `&gt;= X.Y` and `&lt; (X+1).0`; `~&gt; X.Y.Z` means `&gt;= X.Y.Z` and `&lt; X.(Y+1).0`.</summary>

Version requirement string (e.g., `&gt;= 2.0`, `~&gt; 1.5`, `= 2.1`). The `~&gt;` operator is a pessimistic version constraint: `~&gt; X.Y` means `&gt;= X.Y` and `&lt; (X+1).0`; `~&gt; X.Y.Z` means `&gt;= X.Y.Z` and `&lt; X.(Y+1).0`.

**Type:** `string`

</details>

<details>
<summary><a id="version-requirements"></a><strong><code>version_requirements</code></strong> — Version requirement or list of requirements (e.g., `&gt;= 2.0` or `['&gt;= 2.0', '&lt; 3.0']`)</summary>

Version requirement or list of requirements (e.g., `&gt;= 2.0` or `['&gt;= 2.0', '&lt; 3.0']`)

</details>

<details>
<summary><a id="extension-requirement"></a><strong><code>extension_requirement</code></strong> — A requirement on an extension. Can either specify just an extension name, in which case version '&gt;= 0' is implied, or both a name and a requirement</summary>

A requirement on an extension. Can either specify just an extension name, in which case version '&gt;= 0' is implied, or both a name and a requirement

| Property | Type | Required | Description |
|----------|------|----------|-------------|
| `name` | [`extension_name`](#extension-name) | ✓ |  |
| `version` | [`version_requirements`](#version-requirements) |  |  |

</details>
